name: Adham ibn Umayya
title: Abdi Warrior
shortDescription: A Shia and warrior from Basra who gathered at the house of Mariya bint Munqidh and rushed to assist the Imam.
fullStory:
Adham ibn Umayya al-Abdi was a sincere Shia and warrior from the city of Basra. At a time when Ibn Ziyad had created severe oppression in Basra to prevent anyone from aiding Imam Hussain (AS), the house of a brave Shia woman named 'Mariya bint Munqidh' served as a gathering place for Basran Shias.

Adham ibn Umayya, along with several others including Yazid ibn Thubayt (introduced in previous phases), took an oath in this house to join the Master of Martyrs' caravan. Despite the dangerous and blocked routes, they secretly left Basra.

After a long journey, Adham reached Imam Hussain (AS) in Mecca (or mid-journey) and accompanied him to Karbala.

On the day of Ashura, with the start of the arrow barrage and the enemy's all-out attack in the morning, Adham ibn Umayya manfully entered the battlefield and, after a brave battle, drank the cup of martyrdom in that very first attack, achieving eternal salvation.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى أَدْهَمَ بْنِ أُمَيَّةَ العَبْدِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Adham ibn Umayya al-Abdi.
